changes: OpenClaw shipped v2026.4.2 on 2026-04-02, one day after v2026.4.1, maintaining a very fast category-anchor release tempo.
HiClaw published v1.0.9 on 2026-04-02, showing the orchestration / team-assistant lane is still iterating after the repo move.
SwarmClaw published v1.3.4 on 2026-04-02, reinforcing that the multi-agent long tail is still shipping in public.
Hermes Agent, ZeroClaw, PicoClaw, NanoClaw, IronClaw, AstrBot, and TinyAGI all showed continued repo movement in the 2026-04-03 snapshot without forcing a taxonomy reset.
The monitored set still did not need to change: Hermes remains the canonical tracked repo at https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent, and the current positioning map still holds.
whyItMatters: The clearest fresh signal is continued category velocity: OpenClaw is still shipping often enough to define the pace others react to.
HiClaw and SwarmClaw matter as proof that the multi-agent / orchestration branch is still alive across more than one public project.
This is real movement, but still mostly execution inside existing lanes rather than a strategic redraw of the ecosystem.
That keeps map.md and executive-digest.md stable while today.md records the incremental pulse.
